content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 26, 2002


Marcia H. Armstrong
9216 Smokey Lane
Ft Jones, CA 96032

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-02-165

Dear Ms. Armstrong:

This is in response to your request for assistance under the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").¹ Because your letter does not seek advice regarding any provision of the
Act, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.²


QUESTION AND CONCLUSION

You were elected to the Siskiyou County Board of Supervisors in March 2002.
Your term of office does not begin until January 2003. Are there any restrictions on your
attendance as "supervisor-elect" at social, community and civic events before your term
of office begins in January of 2003?

The Fair Political Practices Commission interprets and enforces the provisions of
the Political Reform Act, which regulates such things as disclosure of campaign
contributions and expenditures during elections, conflict-of-interest issues facing public
officials and financing of lobbying activities before the state Legislature and state
administrative agencies. The question you pose is not under the jurisdiction of the Fair
Political Practices Commission. It is suggested you contact your county counsel for
assistance with this issue.


¹Government Code §§ 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, §§ 18109-18997,
of the California Code of Regulations.
²Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice (Government Code § 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. § 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)

File No. I-02-165
Page 2

You do need to be aware, however, that as an elected officer of a local
governmental agency, you may not receive gifts aggregating more than $320 in value in a
calendar year from a single source. Additionally, when you do assume office in January,
you will disclose on your statement of economic interests any gifts valued at $50 or more
received in the prior 12 months from a single source. Gifts include such things as meals
or tickets to social or sports venues. I have enclosed a fact sheet on the receipt of gifts,
travel and honoraria to assist you further.

If you have any questions please feel free to contact me at (866) 275-3772.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

Enclosures
